content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Bad Luck Island". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/540/problem/D
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of islands. Then n lines follow, each containing two integers xi and yi, the coordinates of the i-th island.
content.outputFormat: The output should be a single number, the minimum number of moves required to visit all islands and return to the starting point.

code.Python3:
import math
n = int(input())
x = [0] * n
y = [0] * n
for i in range(n):
    x[i], y[i] = map(int, input().split())
current_x, current_y = x[0], y[0]
total_distance = 0
for i in range(1, n):
    dx = x[i] - current_x
    dy = y[i] - current_y
    total_distance += math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy)
    current_x, current_y = x[i], y[i]
dx = x[0] - current_x
 dy = y[0] - current_y
total_distance += math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy)
print(total_distance)
